Home / Services / Interior Carpentry

Interior Carpentry

Custom woodwork that honors your home's character while adding modern function.

From restoring original 1920s built-ins in Brookside to crafting closet systems for modern Northland homes, our carpenters bridge Kansas City's architectural eras with precision craftsmanship.

We mill replacement trim to match historic profiles, using local lumber when possible to maintain grain consistency. For renovations, we ensure new work blends seamlessly - whether that means distressing maple to match century-old oak or replicating Craftsman-era joinery.

Common projects include:
- Window/door casings damaged by seasonal movement
- Built-in bookshelves and window seats
- Wainscoting and crown molding installations
- Pantry and closet organization systems

For newer homes, we install sturdy shelving systems that withstand kids and pets. For historic properties, we document original details before making alterations, preserving your home's story.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Assess existing architecture and take precise measurements
  2. 02 Step 2: Select materials matching original species/grain where applicable
  3. 03 Step 3: Fabricate components in our climate-controlled shop
  4. 04 Step 4: Install with period-appropriate fasteners/finishes

Benefits

  • Maintains historic home authenticity
  • Uses durable hardwoods suited to Midwest humidity
  • Custom solutions for awkward spaces
  • Precision joinery prevents seasonal gaps

Frequently Asked Questions

Can you match my home's original trim exactly?

We maintain profiles for most KC architectural styles and can create custom knives for router work if needed. Stains are custom-mixed on site.

Will new woodwork shrink/gap in winter?

We kiln-dry all lumber to 6-8% moisture content - the ideal range for our climate - minimizing seasonal movement.

Get a Free Estimate

Ready to get started with interior carpentry? Contact us today.
